description_long: This object is a short sword or dagger featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, crafted from a dark metallic material. The crossguard is broad and flat, made of brass or bronze, providing hand protection and a visual contrast to the blade. The handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly toward the pommel, constructed from a reddish-brown wood or wood-like material, capped with a rounded metallic pommel. The overall design is simple yet functional, with no visible engravings or decorative elements. The materials and proportions suggest it is intended for practical use rather than ceremonial display.
description: A short, double-edged sword or dagger with a dark metal blade, brass crossguard, and a wooden handle capped by a round pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the blade, crossguard, and the rounded pommel atop the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle and pommel.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slimness of the blade and the handle's profile.
attribute_handle_design: tapered cylindrical wooden handle with a rounded metallic pommel
